title: [WIP] Replace all docker hub references with quay
body:
#### What type of PR is this?

/kind flake

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

***Depends on:*** https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/4828

Previously tests relied heavily on magic present in
`tests/registries.conf`.  This is less than intuitive and potentially
surprising to test developers.  Search down and replace all test
references to docker.io images with quay.io/libpod images.

#### How to verify it

CI will pass

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [cevich] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Looking at one single failure:
```
not ok 100 bud with --layers and --no-cache flags
...
#/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
#|     FAIL: buildah inspect --format {{index .Docker.History 2}} test1
#| expected: =~ 'FROM quay.io/libpod/alpine:latest'
#|   actual:    '[....] FROM docker.io/library/alpine:latest false}'
```
Context: this is the Dockerfile:
```
FROM alpine
RUN mkdir /hello
...
```
And this is the relevant magic from `/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf `:
```
"alpine" = "docker.io/library/alpine"
```
This means that `FROM alpine` becomes `FROM docker.io/library/alpine`. It is IMHO insane for us to muck with the shortnames file.

From there, the registries.conf *invisibly* translates `docker.blah` to `quay.blah`. "Invisibly" means "this does not echo in the buildah output".

There's a reason I gave up on my attempt to fix this all those years ago. My super-strong preference was and still is to use FQINs everywhere, but that ended up introducing other nightmares, and I just gave up. Kudos to you for persevering, but I'm sorry, I don't know what to suggest here. I will admire you if you pick at each failure and fix it, I will also admire you if you toss your hands up in despair and run away. This is gory stuff.

> It is IMHO insane for us to muck with the shortnames file.

Ahh okay, I guess that was my sticking point.  I thought maybe there was a chance it was put in place by some test-related script somewhere.

> There's a reason I gave up on my attempt to fix this all those years ago.

Oh, you tried this already and gave up?  Hmmmm, that makes me less inclined to muck with it further then.

Okay -sigh-, "magic" it is then.
